questions: What are the symptoms of flu?
What to do if I have pneumonia?
I have fever and cough, what could it be?
Shortness of breath, when to seek help?
answers: Common symptoms of flu include fever, cough, fatigue, headache, and sore throat.
For migraine, rest in a quiet dark room, avoid bright lights and loud noise. If severe, consult a doctor.
Pneumonia requires medical attention, especially with chest pain or difficulty breathing.
If no symptoms match concerning conditions, you appear healthy. Maintain good habits.
COVID-19 symptoms include fever, cough, fatigue, loss of smell, and shortness of breath.
Fever and cough could indicate flu or respiratory infection. Monitor symptoms and consult if worsening.
Headache and fatigue might be migraine or general illness. Rest and hydrate.
Shortness of breath is serious; seek immediate medical help if persistent.
